title: Xtra Light Malaysian Rice Flour Bao
description: A fresh and shaggy Malaysian dish featuring pink food coloring, made with long-fermentation dough, suitable for Buddhist temple gatherings.
instructions: Combine rice flour, sugar, yeast, and salt in a large mixing bowl.
Gradually add in water and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Add pink food coloring and mix until the color is evenly distributed.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment in a warm place for 4 hours.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Brush the tops with vegetable oil and sesame oil.
Steam the buns over boiling water for 12 minutes.
